body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` **Triggered by:** PR #57895 (`ecd2108`) by @RaviPidaparthi ## What Changed PR #57895 added convenience generators to `ResponseEventStream` — `OutputItemMessage()` (string and `IAsyncEnumerable` overloads), `OutputItemFunctionCall()`, and `OutputItemReasoningItem()` — and restructured Samples 3, 4, and 6 with a convenience-first pattern. The changes promote convenience generators as the recommended approach for most `ResponseEventStream` use cases, relegating the full builder API to advanced scenarios only. ## Gaps Found - The `README.md` **Key Concepts > ResponseEventStream** section shows the full builder pattern (`stream.AddOutputItemMessage()`, `message.EmitAdded()`, etc.) as the primary example, with no mention of the new convenience generators (`stream.OutputItemMessage()`) - The README description "Use `ResponseEventStream` when you need multiple output types … fine-grained delta control … custom Response properties" no longer reflects the convenience-first hierarchy introduced in this PR - The README omits the streaming tokens overload `stream.OutputItemMessage(IAsyncEnumerable)`, which is now a first-class supported pattern documented in Sample 3 - The three-tier mental model (TextResponse → convenience generators → full builder) is fully documented in `samples/Sample3_FullControlResponseStream.md` but absent from the package README that most users read first
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File to edit:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` In the **Key concepts > ResponseEventStream** section, replace the current block that reads: > **`ResponseEventStream` — full control over every SSE event:** > > Use `ResponseEventStream` when you need multiple output types (reasoning, function calls), fine-grained delta control, or custom Response properties. > > ` ```C# Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_EchoHandler_FullControl ` with content that introduces the three-tier hierarchy: 1. `TextResponse` — simple text-only responses (already documented, no change) 2. `ResponseEventStream` with **convenience generators** — recommended for most non-text-only scenarios (NEW: `OutputItemMessage`, `OutputItemFunctionCall`, `OutputItemReasoningItem`) 3. `ResponseEventStream` with **full builder control** — for advanced scenarios (custom item properties, interleaved non-event work, multiple summary parts) The convenience generator example to add is already captured in snippet `Responses_Sample3_GreetingHandlerConvenience`: ```csharp public class GreetingHandler : ResponseHandler { public override async IAsyncEnumerable CreateAsync( CreateResponse request, ResponseContext context, [EnumeratorCancellation] CancellationToken cancellationToken) { await Task.CompletedTask; var stream = new ResponseEventStream(context, request); stream.Response.Temperature = 0.7; yield return stream.EmitCreated(); yield return stream.EmitInProgress(); foreach (var evt in stream.OutputItemMessage($"Hello! You said: \"{request.GetInputText()}\"")) yield return evt; yield return stream.EmitCompleted(); } } ``` Also add or reference the streaming-tokens overload from snippet `Responses_Sample3_StreamingGreetingHandler`: ```csharp // Stream tokens as they arrive — each chunk becomes a delta event. await foreach (var evt in stream.OutputItemMessage( GenerateTokensAsync(request.GetInputText(), cancellationToken), cancellationToken)) { yield return evt; } ``` Keep the existing full-control builder snippet (`Responses_ReadMe_EchoHandler_FullControl`) below, clearly labeled as "With full event control" for advanced scenarios. Update the prose description to match Sample 3's explanation: > Use convenience generators when you need to emit function calls, reasoning items, or multiple outputs but don't need per-event control. Drop down to the builder API only when you need to set custom properties on output items before `EmitAdded()`, interleave non-event work between individual delta/done calls, or emit multiple summary parts within a single reasoning item. ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The snippet for the convenience generator approach should be written in a test file so the `Update-Snippets.ps1` tool can inject it into the README automatically. **Test file:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Samples/SampleReadme.cs` (create or edit — check if a ReadMe sample test file already exists; if not, create one following the pattern in `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/tests/samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs`) Add a test method wrapping the convenience generator example: ```csharp [Test] public async Task ReadMe_EchoHandler_Convenience() { #region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_EchoHandler_Convenience // Use OutputItemMessage() for non-text-only responses without per-event control. // The convenience generator handles all inner events automatically. public class GreetingHandler : ResponseHandler { public override async IAsyncEnumerable CreateAsync( CreateResponse request, ResponseContext context, [EnumeratorCancellation] CancellationToken cancellationToken) { await Task.CompletedTask; var stream = new ResponseEventStream(context, request); yield return stream.EmitCreated(); yield return stream.EmitInProgress(); var input = request.GetInputText(); foreach (var evt in stream.OutputItemMessage($"Hello! You said: \"{input}\"")) yield return evt; yield return stream.EmitCompleted(); } } #endregion await Task.CompletedTask; } ``` **README placeholder** to insert in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md`, under the `ResponseEventStream` key concept, after the prose introduction of convenience generators: ```` ```C# Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_EchoHandler_Convenience ``` ```` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README currently has all required sections. Only the **Key Concepts > ResponseEventStream** subsection needs to be updated: | Section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started — Install the package | ✅ Present | | Getting started — Prerequisites | ✅ Present | | Getting started — Configure the server | ✅ Present | | Key concepts — ResponseHandler | ✅ Present | | Key concepts — TextResponse | ✅ Present | | Key concepts — ResponseEventStream (convenience generators) | ❌ **Missing** | | Key concepts — ResponseEventStream (full builder) | ✅ Present (but needs updated guidance text) | | Key concepts — Streaming & Background Modes | ✅ Present | | Key concepts — Response Lifecycle | ✅ Present | | Examples | ✅ Present | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | | Next steps | ✅ Present | | Contributing | ✅ Present | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirm README was updated with the new snippet 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no new public API was added in this doc change, but run to confirm no regressions 5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [AgentServer] Docs: README Key Concepts missing convenience generators for ResponseEventStream
